note: Although there is some overlap with the later 10th c. CE Lewis Palestinian Syriac Lectionary in the case of Genesis 8–9:6; Psalms 76:1; Proverbs 9:7–10; Jonah 4:11, Joel 3:1b–6, 4:6–8; and Deuteronomy 10:12–17, this text represents a previously unknown early version of the Old Testament Lectionary, with significant textual variants and a different selection of biblical lessons. Some biblical passages are shorter in the Jerusalem Armenian Lectionary 121; Exodus 6:28-7:9 and Psalms 83:1, present in this version, are not part of the Armenian Lectionary

bib.citation: Lewis, Agnes Smith, ed. A Palestinian Syriac Lectionary containing lessons from the Pentateuch, Job, Proverbs, Acts and Epistles London: C.J. Clay and Sons, 1897
Duensing, Hugo. Christlich-palästinisch-aramäische Texte und Fragmente. Göttingen: Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, 1906
Lewis, Agnes Smith and Margaret Dunlop Gibson. Supplement to a Palestinian Syriac Lectionary. Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1907
Roux, A. Le codex arménien Jérusalem 121. Patrologia Orientalis 163, 32.2. Turnhout: Brepols, 1971
Müller-Kessler, Christa. "The Early Lectionary Tradition in Christian Palestinian Aramaic: Lections Containing Unattested Old Testament Passages (Sinai, Greek NF MG 32)." Le Muséon 136:1–2 (2023)

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
